title: What’s one investing lesson that really changed your approach?
author: Eleanor_Watson9
selftext:
I’m 36 now, and this feels like an important stage in my investing journey

A lot of what I’ve learned came from talking with other investors over the years — discussing strategy, valuation, market trends, risk, and different ways of looking at opportunities

Some focused on value, some on growth, some on macro, and some on sentiment. What helped me most wasn’t finding one “correct” style. It was learning how different people build a process and stay disciplined

I’m grateful for that, and it reminded me how valuable open discussion can be when people are genuinely trying to improve

I’m not promoting anything — just wanted to ask: what’s one lesson or mindset shift that made you a better investor?

comments.author: japicbens
comments.body: Move abroad to retire in my 40s

comments.author: zarijn_paulo
comments.body: Well any good info helps me later when i get to a point where those strategies can help. Currently i am a deer in headlights with family members pushing me into time wasting stocks that fluctuate like crazy. And one says rhe other one is wrong. At my wits end with them. I'm Interested

replies.author: True-Brain7967
replies.body: If you’re trying to invest literally just buy index funds and call it a day. It’s the most simple approach and likely them most profitable if you’re not willing to commit time and hard work

comments.author: Alexa_Perezz
comments.body: I'm interested in your strategy. Would you mind sharing it?

comments.body: Get the most assets you can buy don't be greedy.

comments.author: anemonaeae
comments.body: Patience pays. Learned that lesson over and over and over again.
